text: It's been really important to come and have a look today because I see reports, updates, and they talk about numbers. But actually, on the ground, you can sort of move from that sort of summary of what's going on to look at the actual impact on the community and the people, and hearing their stories about what they were doing the day that the rain came, what took place after that, how that's impacting their lives. See, our Suncorp people involved in restoring not just their homes but their lives, getting them back on the ground, which is fantastic. Behind the facade of the houses, when you come inside, what may have only been a metre of water has basically destroyed most of the infrastructure of the home. Just talking to a couple of the builders, the work that they're doing, what they've achieved, ripping tiles up, replacing walls, and converting, literally in a couple of weeks, into something that's going to be someone's home again, is amazing. So from start to finish, very, very organised, very systematic, and I'm very proud of what everyone's doing on the ground. You know, if you didn't think we were making a difference, we certainly are here.
